text.text: 📉 *Dip detected — third consecutive zero-traffic day (Jun 23–25); manual GA4 tracking check recommended*

*Top Pages* (vs last week)
No page views recorded yesterday.

*Referral Sources* (vs last week)
No sessions recorded yesterday.

*Signals*
• *What:* 0 sessions, 0 users, 0 page views on Jun 25 (vs 22 sessions / 15 users / 24 views on Thu Jun 18 — ↓100% WoW). Jun 24 was also 0; this is the third consecutive zero-traffic day (Jun 23–25).
  *So what:* Three straight zero days is unusual even for an early-stage site and suggests a possible GA4 tracking disruption rather than organic variation. The 22-session baseline on the same weekday keeps this below the formal 30-session alert threshold, so no ticket is auto-created — but the streak warrants a manual check today.
  *Now what:* Open the site and use GA4 DebugView (or browser DevTools → Network → filter gtag) to confirm tracking fires on `/`. If the tag is missing or returning errors, re-deploy with a tracking fix as a priority.

_No ticket — informational only (baseline 22 sessions < 30-session alert floor). Consecutive zero days flagged for human review._

elements.text: Compared against same weekday last week (Thu Jun 18). GA4 data may lag 24–48h. Sheet last updated: 2026-06-26 07:34 Taipei.
